client.destination: Morocco (Grand Tour: Casablanca → Chefchaouen → Fès → Merzouga → Ouarzazate → Marrakech)
client.reference: YS Travel Morocco 11D adhoc (Giant Tour is competitor) November 2026 dep

itinerary.title: Arrival in Casablanca — The Economic Capital
activities: Airport pickup and VIP transfer to hotel
Panoramic city tour
Royal Palace of Casablanca (exterior photography)
Habbous quarter exploration
Mohamed V Square
Welcome dinner at the legendary Rick's Café
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Mohammed V International Airport, our elegant transfer welcomes you to Morocco's vibrant economic capital. After checking into the refined Movenpick, embark on a discovery of Casablanca's architectural contrasts — from the imperial grandeur of the Royal Palace to the artisanal charm of the Habbous quarter. As evening falls, step into the timeless atmosphere of Rick's Café, where cinema history meets gastronomic excellence, setting an unforgettable tone for your Moroccan odyssey.

itinerary.title: Casablanca → Rabat → Chefchaouen — The Blue Pearl
activities: Hassan II Mosque (guided interior visit)
Drive to Rabat — the imperial capital
Royal Palace of Rabat (exterior)
Hassan Tower & Mohammed V Mausoleum with Royal Cavalry
Lunch at Marina Sla (fresh fish)
Scenic journey to Chefchaouen through the Rif Mountains
itinerary.description: Begin with a privileged tour of the monumental Hassan II Mosque, an architectural masterpiece rising dramatically from the Atlantic shore. Continue to Rabat, where colonial elegance meets imperial heritage — pause before the Royal Cavalry guarding the Mohammed V Mausoleum, then savor the catch of the day at Marina Sla. The afternoon unveils the magic of the Rif Mountains as you ascend to Chefchaouen, where the fabled blue medina awaits, cradled in a setting of breathtaking natural beauty.

itinerary.title: Chefchaouen → Volubilis → Meknes → Fès
activities: Guided exploration of Chefchaouen's blue medina
Drive through Rif Mountains to Volubilis
Volubilis UNESCO site — guided tour of Roman ruins
Traditional lunch at Roman City restaurant
Meknes: Bab Mansour Gate & City Walls (photo stop)
Arrival in Fès, the spiritual capital
itinerary.description: Wander through the photogenic blue-washed alleys of Chefchaouen at their most luminous, then descend toward the majestic Roman ruins of Volubilis, a UNESCO treasure framed by golden olive groves. After a traditional Moroccan lunch with mosaics as your dining companions, pass through the imperial city of Meknes to admire the grandeur of Bab Mansour. Your journey culminates in Fès, Morocco's spiritual soul, where the refined Palais Medina Riad Resort opens its doors to an evening of oriental elegance.

itinerary.title: Fès — Imperial City & Ancient Medina
activities: Full-day guided tour with expert local guide
Bab Bou Jeloud (the Blue Gate)
Karaouine Mosque & Moulay Idriss Mosque (exterior)
Bou Inania Madrasah (interior visit)
Royal Palace of Fès (exterior)
Tannerie terrasses — panoramic view
Lunch at Chinese Restaurant
Afternoon drive to Midelt via Middle Atlas
itinerary.description: Immerse yourself in the labyrinthine medina of Fès el-Bali, the world's largest living medieval Islamic city. With a certified local guide, decode the secrets of the Karaouine Mosque, marvel at the intricate zellige of Bou Inania Madrasah, and witness the timeless tanning pits from panoramic terraces. After lunch, ascend the Middle Atlas — cedar forests and cooler altitudes leading to Midelt, a peaceful mountain town where your comfortable retreat awaits before the great desert crossing.

itinerary.title: Midelt → Erfoud → Merzouga — Gateway to the Sahara
activities: Drive through Ziz Valley & date palm oases
Stop at Ifrane — photo at the iconic Stone Lion
Lunch at Chergui Hotel/Restaurant, Erfoud
Fossil factory visit (exterior)
4x4 transfer to Merzouga dunes (4 pax per vehicle)
Sunset camel ride over the Erg Chebbi dunes
Dinner under the Saharan stars at Kasbah Tombouctou
itinerary.description: The day unfolds as a cinematic journey: the fertile Ziz Valley gives way to the alpine town of Ifrane, its Stone Lion a nod to Morocco's surprising diversity. Lunch in Erfoud precedes the exhilarating 4x4 transfer toward the amber dunes of Erg Chebbi. As the sun begins its descent, mount your camel for the iconic ride across the Sahara — a timeless ritual culminating in a silhouette of golden dunes. Evening brings the warm hospitality of Kasbah Tombouctou, where Berber tradition meets refined comfort.

itinerary.title: Merzouga → Tinghir → Dades Valley → Ouarzazate
activities: Scenic drive along the Kasbah Road
Ancient Qanat irrigation system
Tinghir Oasis panoramic viewpoint
Todgha Gorges — walk between towering cliffs
Lunch at Yasmina (chicken tajine with lemon & olives)
Dades Valley & Kelaa M'Gouna (Valley of Roses)
Arrival in Ouarzazate, 'Hollywood of Africa'
itinerary.description: Depart the Sahara along the legendary Road of a Thousand Kasbahs. The ancient Qanat system reveals Morocco's agricultural ingenuity, while the Tinghir Oasis offers postcard panoramas of emerald palm groves set against ochre cliffs. A walk through the Todgha Gorges — their 300-meter walls a natural cathedral — precedes a regional lunch at Yasmina. The afternoon traces the Dades Valley and Valley of Roses, arriving at sunset in Ouarzazate, Morocco's cinematic crossroads where legendary films have been born.

itinerary.title: Ouarzazate → Ait Ben Haddou → Marrakech
activities: Visit Atlas Studios (entrance included)
Ait Ben Haddou UNESCO ksar (exterior & climb to granary)
Lunch at Oasis d'Or restaurant
Drive over the legendary Tizi-N-Tichka pass
Arrival in Marrakech, the Red City
Koutoubia Mosque (exterior)
Dinner at hotel
itinerary.description: Step into cinema history at Atlas Studios, where Gladiator and Game of Thrones came alive. Continue to Ait Ben Haddou — the UNESCO-listed earthen citadel that has starred in countless films, its silhouette as timeless as the desert itself. After a memorable lunch, ascend the spectacular Tizi-N-Tichka pass through the High Atlas, a symphony of serpentine roads and snow-capped peaks. Descend into Marrakech as the setting sun paints the Koutoubia minaret in shades of terracotta, ushering you to the refined Adam Park.

itinerary.title: Marrakech → Essaouira Day Trip → Marrakech
itinerary.meal_plan: BB + Gala Dinner at Chez Ali
activities: Scenic drive to the Atlantic coast
Argan oil cooperative visit (women's collective)
Essaouira fortified medina, harbor, ramparts
Fresh seafood lunch at Zahra Grill
Fish market & fortress exploration
Return to Marrakech
Gala dinner at Chez Ali — Fantasia show
itinerary.description: Escape to the Atlantic breeze of Essaouira, the 'Wind City' beloved by artists since Jimi Hendrix. En route, witness the artisanal production of argan oil by a women's cooperative — a gesture of support for local empowerment. The Portuguese-influenced ramparts frame a harbor of azure fishing boats, leading to a fresh seafood feast at Zahra Grill (octopus, oysters, grilled fish). Evening brings a grand finale: the legendary Fantasia show at Chez Ali, where Berber horsemen, couscous, and pastilla au lait celebrate Moroccan grandeur.

itinerary.title: Marrakech — Highlights & Departure
activities: Bahia Palace (interior visit with local guide)
Majorelle Garden (entrance included)
Horse carriage ride through the medina (4 pax per carriage)
Jemaa el-Fna square
Airport transfer for international departure
itinerary.description: Crown your Moroccan journey with the finest jewels of Marrakech. The Bahia Palace enchants with its painted cedar ceilings and serene Andalusian courtyards, while Majorelle Garden — once Yves Saint Laurent's sanctuary — explodes in cobalt blue amid rare botanicals. Board a horse-drawn carriage for a romantic tour of the medina, circling Jemaa el-Fna's timeless theater. A seamless transfer to Marrakech Menara Airport closes this premium Moroccan odyssey, already a cherished memory.

main_vehicle.model: MAN Irizar i6 / Mercedes Irizar i6

inclusions: 09 days / 08 nights accommodation in listed hotels (or similar)
07 lunches and 08 dinners as per Catering section
1 large bottle mineral water per 3 pax per meal
Complimentary single room for tour leader (1 FOC)
48-seater coach with A/C, Wi-Fi and reclining seats
English-speaking tour guide throughout the journey
Local licensed guides in Fès, Meknes, Rabat, Marrakech (mandatory for 20+ groups)
All monument entrance fees as indicated
Sunset camel ride over the Erg Chebbi dunes
Horse carriage tour in Marrakech (4 pax/carriage)
Gala dinner with Fantasia show at Chez Ali
City taxes and hotel porter tips (in/out)
exclusions: Tips for guides and drivers (recommended: USD 3-5 per pax per day)
Optional activities not mentioned in the program
Any services not specifically mentioned as included

stours_advantages: Premium 5* hotels in Casablanca, Fès & Marrakech (often 4* in competitor packages)
English-speaking guide throughout (not just local guides)
Gala dinner at iconic Chez Ali included
30+ years of DMC expertise in Morocco
Licensed local guides in each imperial city
